summary: Luhn's patent (filed 1954, granted 23 August 1960) describes a hand-operated device for computing and verifying a mod-10 check digit: every second digit from the right is doubled, digits above 9 are reduced by 9, and the total must be divisible by 10. This 'double-add-double' formula is the origin of the Luhn algorithm used for payment card numbers, IMEIs, and many national identifiers. It detects all single-digit errors and most adjacent transpositions but provides no cryptographic integrity.
title: Computer for Verifying Numbers
